name: How long does the water damage restoration process usually take?

mainEntity.name: How long does the water damage restoration process usually take?

acceptedAnswer.text: Water damage restoration typically takes 3 to 7 days for drying, but full repairs can extend to 1 to 3 weeks depending on damage severity. The process includes water extraction (1–2 days), drying (3–7 days), cleaning (1–2 days), and reconstruction (1–3 weeks). Larger jobs or contaminated water may require longer timelines. Most projects take between 3 to 5 days to dry properly. Timelines vary based on material types and saturation levels.
acceptedAnswer.description: Drying takes 3–7 days; full restoration with repairs can take 1–3 weeks depending on damage extent and water contamination level.

description: Drying takes 3–7 days; full restoration with repairs can take 1–3 weeks depending on damage extent and water contamination level.
